corridors.note: Pilot corridor. Denver to Salt Lake City via the Colorado Rockies (Eisenhower Tunnel, Vail Pass, Glenwood Canyon), across the Utah San Rafael Swell, then I-15 north from Cove Fort. Entirely missing from the map today. Waypoints pin ORS onto I-70 so it does not shortcut to the faster I-80 route.

waypoints.note: Silverthorne -- I-70 exit 205, forces the mountain route not I-80
Glenwood Springs -- I-70 through Glenwood Canyon
Green River, Utah -- I-70 east of the San Rafael Swell
Salina, Utah -- I-70 west of the Swell
Cove Fort -- I-70 ends at I-15
Nephi -- I-15 north toward Salt Lake City

corridors.note: Montana I-15: Butte north through Helena and Great Falls to Shelby (US-2 junction near the Canada line). Uncovered; Great Falls and Helena are big missing freight cities. Sets up the US-2 Hi-Line tie-in.

waypoints.note: Helena -- I-15 through the state capital
Shelby -- I-15 at US-2 near the Canada line

corridors.note: The Hi-Line: US-2 across the northern tier of Montana and North Dakota, Shelby to Grand Forks. No interstate shadows it (I-94 is far south), so it passes the shadow rule. Loaded with uncovered freight cities -- Williston (Bakken oil), Minot, Havre, Glasgow.
I-90 Billings southeast through the Wyoming Powder River Basin (Sheridan, Gillette) to Rapid City. Uncovered coal-and-oil freight country; both endpoints are existing nodes.
US-2 Hi-Line eastern half: Grand Forks across northern Minnesota to Duluth, completing the corridor into the existing Duluth hub.

waypoints.note: Grand Forks -- US-2 east start (existing node)

corridors.note: US-2 western half: Shelby over Marias Pass through NW Montana (Kalispell) and the Idaho panhandle (Sandpoint) to Spokane. Uncovered; Kalispell is a real Flathead Valley freight hub.

US-2 westernmost segment: Spokane across the Columbia Plateau to Wenatchee, then over Stevens Pass to Everett on Puget Sound -- completing US-2 coast to coast. Wenatchee is a real central-Washington freight hub.

waypoints.note: Everett -- US-2 at Puget Sound (existing node)

corridors.note: I-94 across eastern Montana into North Dakota: Billings to Bismarck along the Yellowstone valley. Existing map skips straight across with billings-dickinson and billings-bismarck long legs; Miles City and Glendive are the real freight towns on the road. The southern-tier counterpart to the US-2 Hi-Line, completing the MT/ND grid.

waypoints.note: Miles City -- I-94, forces the eastern route not I-90
Glendive -- I-94 near the ND line

corridors.note: I-80 across the Nevada Great Basin: Reno to Salt Lake City. Today the map skips it with a single reno-slc leg and leaves Elko hanging off SLC only (no reno-elko), so Reno->Elko backtracks. Real freight/mining towns on the road: Winnemucca, Battle Mountain, Elko (exists), West Wendover at the Utah line.

waypoints.note: West Wendover -- I-80 at the Utah line
Salt Lake City -- I-80 end (existing node)

corridors.note: I-84 east half over the Oregon Blue Mountains: Pendleton up Cabbage Hill and down to Boise. Today portland-boise skips the whole east half and Pendleton dead-ends short of it. Real freight cities on the road: La Grande, Baker City, Ontario at the Snake River (Idaho line).

waypoints.note: La Grande -- I-84 over the Blue Mountains
Ontario -- I-84 at the Idaho line

corridors.note: US-101 up the Northern California coast: Santa Rosa through Ukiah and the redwoods to Eureka on Humboldt Bay, then north to Crescent City near the Oregon line. The whole North Coast is a dead zone today. Spurs (added directly) tie Eureka east to Redding on CA-299 and Crescent City north to Grants Pass on US-199, plugging the coast into I-5.

waypoints.note: Santa Rosa -- US-101 south start (existing node)
Crescent City -- US-101 near the Oregon line

corridors.note: I-75 through Appalachian Kentucky: Lexington south through the Cumberland foothills to Knoxville. No lexington-knoxville leg exists today -- a virgin build connecting the Bluegrass to East Tennessee. Real freight towns on the road: Richmond, Berea, London, Corbin.

waypoints.note: Corbin -- I-75 near the Cumberland Gap country

corridors.note: I-64 down the Ohio River country: Lexington east through Morehead to the tri-state at Ashland (KY) and Huntington (WV), then on to Charleston. Entirely missing today -- lexington-charleston does not exist. Ashland is the US-23 gateway to the Eastern Kentucky coalfields (next corridor).

waypoints.note: Ashland -- I-64 at the Ohio, US-23 junction

corridors.note: Eastern Kentucky coalfields: down US-23 (the Country Music Highway) from Ashland through Prestonsburg to Pikeville, then west on the KY-80 Hal Rogers Parkway through Hazard to London on I-75. Ties the coal country into the map via Ashland (I-64) and London (I-75). Real Appalachian coal-and-log freight.

waypoints.note: Pikeville -- US-23 Pike County coal hub

corridors.note: The Oregon coast: US-101 from Coos Bay north through Newport to Astoria at the mouth of the Columbia. Whole coast is a dead zone. Ties inland to I-5 via Coos Bay-Roseburg (OR-42) and Astoria-Portland (US-30), added directly.

waypoints.note: Coos Bay -- US-101 south end, Oregon's largest coastal port
Newport -- US-101 central coast, Yaquina Bay
Astoria -- US-101 at the Columbia (US-30 to Portland)

corridors.note: I-80 up the Platte River valley across western Nebraska: Grand Island to Cheyenne. Grand Island, North Platte, and Cheyenne exist; the long grand_island-north_platte and north_platte-cheyenne legs skip the real I-80 towns (Kearney, Lexington, Ogallala, Sidney).

waypoints.note: Grand Island -- I-80 east start (existing node)

corridors.note: I-70 across Kansas and onto the Colorado high plains: Topeka through Salina to Denver. Topeka-Salina exists but skips Junction City; west of Salina (Hays, Colby, and on across eastern Colorado) is entirely missing. The famous flat Kansas City-Denver express leg stays.

I-10 across West Texas: San Antonio through Kerrville, Junction, Ozona, and Fort Stockton to Van Horn and El Paso. The 555-mile San Antonio-El Paso express and the 456-mile Van Horn-San Antonio skip were replaced by the real desert chain; the US-90 Del Rio border alternate stays. Built.

waypoints.note: San Antonio -- I-10 west start (existing node)
El Paso -- I-10 west end (existing node)

corridors.note: I-25 over Raton Pass: Pueblo through Walsenburg, Trinidad, and Raton to Las Vegas, New Mexico, closing the 214-mile Las Vegas-Pueblo skip. Raton Pass carries the real ~6% grade and the New Mexico-Colorado state line. The pinned Denver-Albuquerque US-285 mountain express is untouched. Built.

waypoints.note: Trinidad -- I-25 Colorado side of Raton Pass
Raton -- I-25 New Mexico side of the pass
Las Vegas, NM -- I-25 south end (existing node)

corridors.note: I-5 over the Siskiyous: split the 149-mile Redding->Medford hop (and drop the redundant 219-mile Medford->Chico express) by adding Mount Shasta and Yreka. The Yreka->Medford leg carries Siskiyou Summit and the California-Oregon state line; the climb past Dunsmuir and Black Butte is real mountain grade. Built.

waypoints.note: Mount Shasta -- I-5 below the peak
Medford -- I-5 over Siskiyou Summit (existing node)

corridors.note: I-29 up the Dakotas: add Watertown to split the 206-mile Fargo->Brookings hop (Brookings already existed), completing Fargo -> Watertown -> Brookings -> Sioux Falls. Also dropped two redundant unprotected expresses: the 248-mile Fargo->Sioux Falls and the 425-mile Fargo->Omaha. The Fargo->Watertown leg carries the North Dakota-South Dakota line. Built.

Willamette Valley fill: the I-5 spine (Portland-Salem-Albany-Eugene, with Albany tying Corvallis in) already existed; this adds Woodburn on I-5 and the OR-99W wine-country western alternate through Newberg and McMinnville, giving a distinct Portland->Salem route. Dropped the now-redundant Portland-Salem, Eugene-Salem, and Eugene-Portland expresses. Rex Hill on 99W comes out hills; the rest is flat valley floor. Built.
